message:
feat(lessons): enhance DCG lesson and add DCG to safety-tools-lesson

- Update DCG lesson with improved content and simplified icons
- Add DCG section to safety-tools-lesson alongside SLB and CAAM
- Update GoalBanner to mention DCG, SLB, and CAAM
- Include DCG commands and tips in safety tools overview

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Learn to use DCG to block dangerous commands before they execute. + Use DCG to block destructive commands before they do damage. - {/* What Is DCG */}
} delay={0.1} > - DCG (Destructive Command Guard) is a safety net - that catches dangerous commands before they can cause damage. It works - as a Claude Code hook, intercepting commands in real-time. + DCG (Destructive Command Guard) is a Claude + Code hook that blocks dangerous commands before they execute. It + protects your repos from hard resets, recursive deletes, destructive + database commands, and more. - Think of it as a guard at the door who checks every command before - letting it through. Dangerous patterns like rm -rf /,{" "} - git reset --hard, or DROP TABLE get blocked - instantly. + Think of it as a safety interlock: if a command looks destructive, + DCG stops it and suggests a safer alternative.
} - title="Sub-50ms Latency" - description="Blocks in milliseconds, never slows you down" - gradient="from-yellow-500/20 to-amber-500/20" + icon={} + title="Pre-Execution Blocking" + description="Stops damage before it happens" + gradient="from-red-500/20 to-rose-500/20" + /> + } + title="Protection Packs" + description="Git, filesystem, database, cloud, and more" + gradient="from-amber-500/20 to-orange-500/20" /> } - title="50+ Protection Packs" - description="Git, filesystem, database, K8s, cloud" + icon={} + title="Allow-Once Codes" + description="Explicit bypass when you know it is safe" gradient="from-primary/20 to-violet-500/20" /> } + icon={} title="Fail-Open Design" - description="Never blocks your workflow on errors" + description="Errors never block your workflow" gradient="from-emerald-500/20 to-teal-500/20" /> - } - title="Allow-Once Bypass" - description="Legitimate bypasses with time-limited codes" - gradient="from-red-500/20 to-rose-500/20" - />
- {/* Why DCG Matters */}
} + title="How DCG Intercepts Commands" + icon={} delay={0.15} > - -
-
- -
-
-

- AI agents occasionally make destructive mistakes. -

-

- Hours of uncommitted work can vanish in seconds. DCG catches - these mistakes before damage occurs. -

-
-
- + + DCG runs as a PreToolUse hook inside Claude + Code. Every command is checked against a set of rules before it runs. + -
- - - + BLOCKED: git.reset.hard +> Why: hard reset discards uncommitted work +> Safer: git restore --staged .`} + language="bash" />
+ + + If DCG blocks a command, slow down and read the explanation. It is + showing you the dangerous part and a safer path. +
- {/* Essential Commands */}
} @@ -141,176 +115,99 @@ export function DcgLesson() { '", description: "Check if a command would be blocked", }, { - command: "dcg test 'command' --explain", - description: "Get detailed explanation of why", + command: "dcg test '' --explain", + description: "Explain why a command is unsafe", }, { command: "dcg packs", - description: "List all protection packs", + description: "List available protection packs", }, { - command: "dcg packs --enabled", - description: "Show only enabled packs", + command: "dcg install", + description: "Register the Claude Code hook", }, { - command: "dcg doctor", - description: "Check DCG installation health", + command: "dcg allow-once ", + description: "Bypass for a single approved command", }, { - command: "dcg install", - description: "Register Claude Code hook", + command: "dcg doctor", + description: "Check installation and hook status", }, ]} />
- {/* Allow-Once Workflow */}
} + title="Protection Packs" + icon={} delay={0.25} > - Sometimes you legitimately need to run a blocked command. DCG provides - the allow-once workflow for controlled bypasses. + Packs let you enable or disable rules based on your workflow. Keep the + ones you need to avoid false positives. -
- - - -
-
- - {`# Command gets blocked with code ABC-123 -$ dcg allow-once ABC-123 - -# Now the command will be allowed (for 60 seconds)`} - +
-
- - Allow-once codes expire after a short time. Use them promptly and - only when you're sure the command is safe in context. - -
+ + Start with git and{" "} + filesystem packs. Add database or cloud packs + only when you use those tools. +
- {/* DCG + SLB */}
} + title="When You See a Block" + icon={} delay={0.3} > - DCG and SLB work together to provide layered safety: + A block is a warning, not a dead end. Use it as a checkpoint: +
    +
  • 1) Read the explanation carefully.
    • +
  • 2) Prefer the safer alternative when possible.
    • +
  • 3) Use allow-once only if you are confident.
    • +
  • 4) Document the decision in your commit or notes.
    • +
+
-
- - } - title="DCG: Pre-Execution" - description="Blocks obvious destructive patterns instantly" - gradient="from-red-500/20 to-rose-500/20" - /> - } - title="SLB: Post-Proposal" - description="Human approval for contextual risks" - gradient="from-amber-500/20 to-orange-500/20" - /> - -
+ -
- - DCG catches the obvious mistakes. SLB catches the subtle ones that - require human judgment about context and intent. - -
+
} + delay={0.35} + > + + + DCG blocks obvious destructive commands instantly. SLB handles + contextual risk that needs human approval. Together, they form a + layered safety system. + +

- Use SLB and CAAM for safety and account management. + Use DCG, SLB and CAAM for layered safety and account management. {/* Introduction */} @@ -44,16 +45,22 @@ export function SafetyToolsLesson() { > AI agents are powerful but can cause damage if misused. The - Dicklesworthstone stack includes two safety tools: + Dicklesworthstone stack includes three safety tools:
+ } + title="DCG" + description="Pre-execution blocking of destructive commands" + gradient="from-red-500/20 to-rose-500/20" + /> } title="SLB" description="Two-person rule for dangerous commands" - gradient="from-red-500/20 to-rose-500/20" + gradient="from-amber-500/20 to-orange-500/20" /> } @@ -155,6 +162,64 @@ export function SafetyToolsLesson() { + {/* DCG Section */} +
} + delay={0.3} + > + + DCG blocks dangerous commands before they run. + It inspects every command from Claude Code and stops destructive + patterns like hard resets, force pushes, and recursive deletes. + + + If a command is safe, it runs normally. If it's risky, DCG blocks + it and suggests a safer alternative. + + +
+ + Treat a DCG block as a safety checkpoint. Read the explanation and + prefer the safer command whenever possible. + +
+
+ + {/* DCG Commands */} +
} + delay={0.35} + > + ' --explain", + description: "Explain why a command would be blocked", + }, + { + command: "dcg packs", + description: "List available protection packs", + }, + { + command: "dcg install", + description: "Register DCG as a Claude Code hook", + }, + { + command: "dcg allow-once ", + description: "Bypass a single approved command", + }, + { + command: "dcg doctor", + description: "Check installation and hook status", + }, + ]} + /> +
